title: Would You Like A Robot Companion?

blanks.sentence: Robotics - the _____ robots
blanks.evidence: One area of technology which has been in the news a lot recently is robotics – the design and building of robots.

blanks.sentence: These machines assist with household chores and _____
blanks.evidence: Humanoid robots, meaning robots which appear and behave like humans, are being built to help us do everything from washing the dishes to babysitting the kids.

blanks.sentence: Not everyone is comfortable with having _____ in their homes
blanks.evidence: But not everyone feels comfortable with the idea of inviting a human-like robot into their home.

blanks.sentence: Despite this, AI enables robots to form _____ with people
blanks.evidence: With advances in AI, robots are becoming smart enough to develop meaningful relationships with humans.

blanks.sentence: Designer: Grace Brown, an engineer from _____
blanks.evidence: For five months during the Covid pandemic, Australian engineer Grace Brown had no human contact.

blanks.sentence: During the pandemic, she experienced no _____ for five months
blanks.evidence: For five months during the Covid pandemic, Australian engineer Grace Brown had no human contact.

blanks.sentence: Built Abi to offer _____ to those feeling isolated
blanks.evidence: She used this time to build a home companion robot, Abi, with the idea it would provide companionship to other people suffering loneliness.

blanks.sentence: Now manufactures robots for _____ through her company Andromeda
blanks.evidence: Now, Grace runs her own company, Andromeda, building robots specially designed as company for elderly residents in care homes who don't get many visitors.

blanks.sentence: Target users: senior citizens in _____ with few visitors
blanks.evidence: Now, Grace runs her own company, Andromeda, building robots specially designed as company for elderly residents in care homes who don't get many visitors.

blanks.sentence: Personality traits: _____, inquisitive and curious
blanks.evidence: Abi, who's the humanoid robot that we build at Andromeda – people don't expect humanoid robots to have, like, so much personality. She's very, very sassy.

blanks.sentence: Visitors are often _____ by her behaviour
blanks.evidence: And I think when people meet her, they're always, kind of, taken aback by… "Oh, she's, like, got a mind of her own."

blanks.sentence: Appears to possess _____ – acts independently
blanks.answer: mind of her own / a mind
blanks.evidence: And I think when people meet her, they're always, kind of, taken aback by… "Oh, she's, like, got a mind of her own."

blanks.sentence: Can perform entertaining actions like blowing _____
blanks.evidence: Unlike the unpredictable and violent robots which are sometimes portrayed in sci-fi movies, Abi is much more approachable and friendly, even funny – she can blow bubbles from her hand!

blanks.sentence: Height: approximately _____ tall
blanks.evidence: I originally modelled her off the size of, like, a young child – so, like a six, seven-year-old child – so she's about 110cm tall.

blanks.sentence: Similar in size to a _____
blanks.evidence: I originally modelled her off the size of, like, a young child – so, like a six, seven-year-old child – so she's about 110cm tall.

blanks.sentence: Appearance includes _____ and large eyes
blanks.evidence: She's got a whole splash of different colours. She's got, you know, large, expressive eyes.

blanks.sentence: Eyes are highly _____, revealing emotions
blanks.evidence: She's got a whole splash of different colours. She's got, you know, large, expressive eyes.

blanks.sentence: Design inspired by animated characters from _____
blanks.evidence: So, that was – I was very much trying to replicate the kind of approachableness and the endearingness of, like, these different, like, Pixar characters that you'd watch in all of these films.

blanks.sentence: Regular conversations with Abi bring _____ and a sense of community to residents
blanks.evidence: Elderly residents who regularly chat with Abi say the robot companion brings them joy and a sense of community.

blanks.sentence: Regular conversations with Abi bring joy and _____ to residents
blanks.evidence: Elderly residents who regularly chat with Abi say the robot companion brings them joy and a sense of community.

multipleChoice.question: What is Neil's attitude towards having a humanoid robot living with him?
options: A. He feels he already has enough people around him
B. He welcomes having a robot to help with chores
C. He is excited about the idea of a robot companion
D. He worries about the robot's safety and control
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil说 “I really don't want to live with a robot. I have enough people in my house.” （第8-9行）

multipleChoice.question: According to the conversation, what is the intended purpose of building Abi, the robot?
options: A. To entertain children by performing tricks and games
B. To provide elderly people with company and reduce loneliness
C. To carry out household cleaning and maintenance tasks
D. To replace human caregivers in nursing homes completely
multipleChoice.evidence: Grace设计Abi是为了陪伴“elderly residents who don't get many visitors”（第26-28行）

multipleChoice.question: In the transcript, what does the word "sassy" imply about Abi's personality?
options: A. She is shy and reserved when meeting people
B. She acts aggressively and unpredictably
C. She is bold, confident, and slightly cheeky
D. She is highly logical and unemotional
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth解释“sassy means bold, confident and cheeky.”（第17-19行）

multipleChoice.question: What does it mean when Grace says Abi “has a mind of her own”?
options: A. Abi always follows human instructions perfectly
B. Abi behaves independently, beyond human control
C. Abi is programmed to obey all commands strictly
D. Abi does not show any personality or curiosity
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil说“mind of her own”指“acting independently of human control.”（第20-21行）

multipleChoice.question: Why did Grace Brown choose to model Abi to look like a young child?
options: A. To make Abi appear approachable and endearing
B. To make Abi seem more powerful and intimidating
C. To minimize the robot's size for easier transportation
D. To ensure Abi blends in with adult users better
multipleChoice.evidence: Grace说“trying to replicate the kind of approachableness and endearingness”像Pixar角色（第35-38行）

multipleChoice.question: Which of the following best expresses the effect Abi has on elderly residents?
options: A. Abi replaces the need for family visits
B. Abi causes confusion and fear among residents
C. Abi brings joy and a feeling of community
D. Abi increases physical activity among the elderly
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil说“Elderly residents ... say the robot companion brings them joy and a sense of community.”（第43-44行）

multipleChoice.question: What contrast does the conversation make between Abi and robots in sci-fi movies?
options: A. Abi is violent and unpredictable like movie robots
B. Abi is designed for military use rather than companionship
C. Abi is friendly and approachable, unlike typical sci-fi robots
D. Abi is less advanced technologically than fictional robots
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth说“Unlike the unpredictable and violent robots in sci-fi movies, Abi is much more approachable and friendly.”（第24-27行）

multipleChoice.question: What reason does Grace give for Abi having large, expressive eyes?
options: A. To allow her to see better in dark places
B. To show emotions and make her feel more relatable
C. To give her a scary appearance to protect children
D. To focus more on surveillance and monitoring tasks
multipleChoice.evidence: Abi有大而“expressive eyes”，即能“show what someone thinks or feels.”（第41-43行）

multipleChoice.question: What can be inferred about people's reaction when they first meet Abi?
options: A. They immediately distrust her technology
B. They expect a robot to lack any personality
C. They find her dull and uninteresting at first
D. They are usually surprised by her confident attitude
multipleChoice.evidence: Grace说人们“are always, kind of, taken aback by ... she’s got a mind of her own.”（第14-16行）

multipleChoice.question: Why is Abi described as having “a splash of colour” in her design?
options: A. To hide mechanical parts from people's view
B. To imitate the colour of human skin tones
C. To camouflage her in care home environments
D. To make her visually attractive and less dull
multipleChoice.evidence: “a splash of colour”用来“brighten up something dull.”（第39-41行）
